description:
From its humble beginnings as a project code-named "Oak" at Sun Microsystems to becoming a global standard for enterprise software and billions of devices, Java's journey is one of radical innovation, strategic pivots, and enduring community strength. 


# Film Patrons

This film was only possible thanks to the incredible support of our sponsors: Oracle, JetBrains, IBM, and Azul. 
 
• *Oracle* : Oracle is the steward of the Java Platform. Learn more at https://dev.java and https://studio.youtube.com/channel/UCmRtPmgnQ04CMUpSUqPfhxQ
• *IBM* : Develop modern applications with the open Java ecosystem and IBM Developer. https://developer.ibm.com/languages/java/
• *JetBrains* : Use IntelliJ IDEA from JetBrains for pro development in Java and Kotlin: https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/
• *Azul* : Azul is the trusted leader in enterprise Java for today's AI and cloud-first world, powering mission-critical systems for 36% of the Fortune 100: https://www.azul.com/
• *Railway* : Railway is the all-in-one intelligent cloud provider ➡️ https://railway.com/?referralCode=cultrepo

# What to Expect from the Film 
 
• *The Birth of Oak* : Discover how  a small team of top engineers at Sun Microsystems originally designed Java to solve problems in computing devices outside the traditional industry scope.
• *Pivoting to the Web* : Learn how the release of the first web browser, Mosaic, inspired the team to rewrite their browser and bring interactivity to the static web using Java.
• *Write Once, Run Anywhere* : Understand the goal of creating a binary platform that could run on any hardware. Revisit the high-stakes legal battles that threatened the "write once, run anywhere" promise.
• *Community and Open Source* : Witness the transition of Java to an open-source platform through the creation of the Java Community Process (JCP) and the release of OpenJDK.
• *The Modern Era* : Explore how Oracle's acquisition of Sun Microsystems impacted the language and whats in store for the future of Java.

# Featuring

*Past & Present Java Team Members from Sun / Oracle*

- *James Gosling* — Creator of Java
- *Joshua Bloch* — Creator of the Java Collections Framework, Author of Effective Java
- *James Duncan Davidson* — Creator of Apache Tomcat
- *Brian Goetz* — Java Language Architect, Java Platform Group, Oracle
- *Tim Lindholm* — Co-author, Java Virtual Machine Specification
- *Kim Polese* — Java's first Product Manager
- *Mark Reinhold* — Chief Architect, Java Platform Group, Oracle
- *Georges Saab* — Senior Vice President, Java Platform Group, Oracle and Chair, OpenJDK Governing Board
- *Paul Sandoz* — Architect, Java Platform Group, Oracle
- *Carla Schroer* — Director of Java Compatibility, Sun Microsystems
- *Sharat Chander* — Senior Director, Java Product Management & Developer Engagement, Oracle
- *Heather VanCura* — Vice President and Java Community Process Chairperson and Director, Oracle

*Java Community*

- *Paul Bakker* — Staff Software Engineer at Netflix 
- *Lars Bergstrom* — Director of Engineering, Google Android 
- *Andrey Breslav* — Creator of Kotlin 
- *Ivar Grimstad* — Jakarta EE Developer Advocate at Eclipse Foundation 
- *Rod Johnson* — Creator of Spring 
- *Gavin King* — Creator of Hibernate 
- *Mark Little* — Vice President Java Runtimes and Java Integration, IBM 
- *Josh Long* — Spring Developer Advocate 
- *Venkat Subramaniam* — Java Champion, Author & Educator 
- *Tagir Valeev* — Technical Lead, Java Team, JetBrains
